Self-reliant tech.<[silence:700ms]> Europe’s digital shield.<[silence:700ms]>

The term tech sovereignty means controlling the tools that power daily life—like a farmer growing their own food instead of relying on distant markets.<[silence:700ms]>

Europe’s push began in 2019, when regulators realized 92 percent of its data was stored on U.S. or Chinese servers.<[silence:700ms]> A single law, the Digital Markets Act (2022), forced Apple and Google to open their app stores to competitors, like a mall requiring all shops to share shelf space.<[silence:700ms]> Meanwhile, the European Processor Initiative funded homegrown chips—such as the Rhea processor—designed for supercomputers, not smartphones.<[silence:700ms]> Why?<[silence:150ms]> So weather forecasts or drug discovery wouldn’t stall if foreign exports stopped.<[silence:700ms]>

Three related subjects are chip fabrication wars, data localization laws, and how trade blocks reshape tech.<[silence:700ms]>
clean_story:
Self-reliant tech. Europe’s digital shield.

The term tech sovereignty means controlling the tools that power daily life—like a farmer growing their own food instead of relying on distant markets.

Europe’s push began in 2019, when regulators realized 92 percent of its data was stored on U.S. or Chinese servers. A single law, the Digital Markets Act (2022), forced Apple and Google to open their app stores to competitors, like a mall requiring all shops to share shelf space. Meanwhile, the European Processor Initiative funded homegrown chips—such as the Rhea processor—designed for supercomputers, not smartphones. Why? So weather forecasts or drug discovery wouldn’t stall if foreign exports stopped.

Three related subjects are chip fabrication wars, data localization laws, and how trade blocks reshape tech.
